description: Dynamic bridge — April 17 session (Friday). Ceasefire extension talks ongoing. April 22 expiry = 4 days. Iran threatens Gulf/Red Sea shipping blockade.

market_regime.label: BLOCKADE_ESCALATION_DIPLOMACY_ACCELERATING
market_regime.key_risk: Blockade ACTIVE + fully implemented. Iran threatens to block Persian Gulf, Sea of Oman, Red Sea shipping if US continues blockade. April 22 ceasefire expiry 4 days away. Ceasefire extension NOT formally agreed.
market_regime.dominant_risk: Iran counter-blockade of Gulf/Red Sea shipping routes. If implemented, Brent spikes $10-15 and Nifty crashes 2-3%. Secondary: ceasefire extension not agreed by weekend.
market_regime.crude_current_zone: WATCH_ZONE ($95-97 — elevated on Iran counter-threats, capped by diplomatic momentum)
market_regime.regime_note: April 16 session: Flat day (-0.14%) after intraday reversal. Opened strong but profit-booking + Iran counter-threats + Friday caution erased gains. HDFC Bank -1.78% dragged indices. Broad midcap/smallcap positive (Adani Power +5.32%, IGIL +5.08%). Gold $4,839 (+0.81%) — war premium sustaining. Brent $95.85 (+0.97%). Key development: Iran threatened Gulf/Red Sea/Sea of Oman shipping blockade in retaliation. Ceasefire extension NOT formally agreed despite Trump optimism. France-UK Hormuz maritime conference Friday.

key_drivers: Nifty closed flat at 24196.75 after intraday reversal — momentum fading
Brent $95.85 slightly elevated (+0.97%) — Iran threatening Red Sea/Gulf shipping blockade
Friday session — natural risk-off ahead of weekend with April 22 ceasefire expiry 4 days away
Ceasefire extension talks ongoing but NOT formally agreed — uncertainty persists
France-UK Hormuz maritime conference Friday adds diplomatic variable
Trump optimism ("war very close to over") provides floor but Iran counter-threats cap upside

session_direction.key_assumption: Weak open (GIFT -0.69%) bought aggressively. RELIANCE +1.58%, HDFC +0.60% recovering, SUZLON +4.34%, IRCON +8.5%, IREDA +3.3%, MRPL +1.81% — strong defense/infra/renewables + heavyweight bid. Friday ahead of April 22 ceasefire deadline. Dip-buying thesis prevails unless Iran escalation signal emerges.

morning_bid_sustains.description: Strong morning breadth (Reliance/defense/renewables) holds. No negative headline. Ceasefire extension chatter keeps bid in. Nifty closes modestly higher.

escalation_signal.description: Iran action on Gulf/Red Sea shipping, or ceasefire extension fails. Brent spikes >$100. Sharp reversal and selloff.
